REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF PETER LEE, MONICA LEE NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION; MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT IS DENIED. PLAINTIFFS FAILED TO SHOW THE LIKELIHOOD OF SUCCESS ON THE MERITS OR IRREPARABLE HARM. THE COURT DOES NOT FIND AT THIS TIME, BASED ON THE EVIDENCE SUBMITTED IN SUPPORT OF THE MOTION, THAT DEFENDANTS HAD ACTUAL OR CONSTRUCTIVE NOTICE OF THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT CONTAINING THE RESTRICTIVE COVENANTS. THERE IS NO ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE DEMONSTRATING THAT JAMESTOWN L.P. DISCLOSED TO DEFENDANTS THE DISCUSSIONS AND/OR AGREEMENT IT HAD WITH PLAINTIFFS, OR THAT IT PROVIDED THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT TO DEFENDANTS PRIOR TO THE PURCHASE. FURTHER, BASED ON THE EVIDENCE SUBMITTED, THE COURT CANNOT CONCLUDE ONE WAY OR THE OTHER, THAT EXHIBIT G, ATTACHED TO THE PURCHASE AND SALES AGREEMENT, PUT DEFENDANTS ON "INQUIRY" NOTICE. AS TO THE HARM, THE NOISE CONCERN IS SPECULATIVE AS THE HVAC UNITS ARE NOT YET OPERABLE. LIKEWISE, PLAINTIFFS CAN ONLY SPECULATE AS TO HOW DEFENDANTS WILL MARKET THE PROPERTY AND DEFENDANTS CLAIM THEY INTEND TO FOLLOW THE LAW. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: VANESSA WU; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Sep-12-2025 Continued To Aug-03-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support
Off Calendar May-12-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

PLAINTIFF PETER LEE, MONICA LEE NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION; MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT is DENIED. Plaintiffs failed to show the likelihood of success on the merits or irreparable harm. The Court does not find at this time, based on the evidence submitted in support of the motion, that Defendants had actual or constructive notice of the settlement agreement containing the restrictive covenants. There is no admissible evidence demonstrating that Jamestown L.P. disclosed to Defendants the discussions and/or agreement it had with Plaintiffs, or that it provided the settlement agreement to Defendants prior to the purchase. Further, based on the evidence submitted, the court cannot conclude one way or the other, that Exhibit G, attached to the Purchase and Sales Agreement, put Defendants on "inquiry" notice. As to the harm, the noise concern is speculative as the HVAC units are not yet operable. Likewise, Plaintiffs can only speculate as to how Defendants will market the property and Defendants claim they intend to follow the law.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-19-2025 Set For Trial On Nov-03-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
